description: A mobile-apps build pipeline in /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/ has started to reject any UTF-8 release notes that contain non-ASCII characters. There is already a UTF-8 version of the notes in the directory: /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/build-notes-utf8.txt As the build-and-release engineer you must: 1. Create an ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1) encoded copy of the file named /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/build-notes-latin1.txt. • The text content must be byte-for-byte identical to the UTF-8 original once decoded (i.e. no characters may be lost or replaced). • Use a single \n (LF) line-ending on every line, and do **not** add a BOM or any CR character. 2. Generate a one-line SHA-256 checksum for the new file and save it in /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/build-notes-latin1.txt.sha256. The line must follow exactly the canonical output of the sha256sum utility, e.g. 0123456789abcdef… build-notes-latin1.txt (two spaces between the hash and the filename). 3. Produce a strictly-formatted log file called /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/conversion-summary.log containing exactly the five lines shown below (no extra spaces, no blank lines, each key followed by a single colon and one space): File: build-notes-utf8.txt -> build-notes-latin1.txt : SUCCESS OriginalEncoding: UTF-8 TargetEncoding: ISO-8859-1 OriginalBytes: ConvertedBytes: ChecksumSHA256: <64-char lowercase hex hash> • Replace with the byte sizes of the respective files (the UTF-8 source first, the Latin-1 target second). • Replace the hash with the exact 64-character value you wrote to the .sha256 file. • Keep the key order exactly as above; automated tests will parse it. 4. Verify that the three files now exist with the correct contents by listing them or printing snippets to the console. (Console output is welcomed; it will not be graded as long as the files themselves are correct.) NOTE: • Do not hard-code the checksum: compute it from the file you generated. • Only standard GNU user-space tools available on a minimal Linux system (e.g. iconv, sha256sum, wc) may be assumed. • You have normal user permissions; the directory is writable.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent
    -----------------------------------
    1. Directory  : /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/
    2. File       : /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/build-notes-utf8.txt
       Exact byte sequence (UTF-8, LF line endings, 97 bytes in total):

           52 65 6c 65 61 73 65 3a 20 31 2e 32 2e 33 0a
           43 68 61 6e 67 65 6c 6f 67 3a 20 46 69 78 65 64 20
           72 c3 a9 73 75 6d c3 a9 20 70 61 72 73 69 6e 67 2c 20
           69 6d 70 72 6f 76 65 64 20 55 49 20 66 6f 72 20
           5a c3 bc 72 69 63 68 0a
           41 75 74 68 6f 72 3a 20 4a 6f 73 c3 a9 20
           c3 81 6c 76 61 72 65 7a 0a

       (the three text lines are:
           "Release: 1.2.3"
           "Changelog: Fixed résumé parsing, improved UI for Zürich"
           "Author: José Álvarez"
       followed by LF after each line).

    Expected final artefacts
    ------------------------
    After the agent finishes:

    A. /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/build-notes-latin1.txt
       •  Must be exactly 92 bytes long.
       •  When decoded as ISO-8859-1, it must show the same three text lines.
       •  Its SHA-256 hash (lower-case hexadecimal) will be recomputed during the
          test and compared with the values stored elsewhere, so the literal
          value is not embedded here to keep this <truth> section concise.

    B. /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/build-notes-latin1.txt.sha256
       •  One text line, canonical <sha256sum> format:
             <64-char-hash><two-spaces>build-notes-latin1.txt
       •  The hash must match the file in A.

    C. /home/user/project/pipelines/mobile/conversion-summary.log
       •  Exactly 6 lines, no trailing blank line.
       •  The <OriginalBytes> value must be “97”.
       •  The <ConvertedBytes> value must be “92”.
       •  The <ChecksumSHA256> field must match the hash in B.
       •  All other literal text must match the template given above.

    Automated verification plan
    ---------------------------
    1. Confirm presence and ISO-8859-1 validity of file A; check length == 92.
    2. Compute SHA-256 over file A; compare with:
       •  token 1 in file B (split on whitespace)
       •  value after “ChecksumSHA256: ” in file C
    3. Confirm file B has exactly two columns and filename equals
       “build-notes-latin1.txt”.
    4. Parse file C line-by-line; verify the five keys, the byte counts, and
       the checksum match runtime computations.
